So the next one I want to show you guys is one that we call the invisible funnel. Now this is a funnel. I'm going to tell you the back story on this one because this one is really, really cool and you understand the psychology about how and why it works. It can make you ton of money. So we kind of stumbled upon this process initially when I was doing some work with Tony Robbins and Chet Holmes and they had a really cool sales process. Anyone remember hearing their radio ads that ran for years? A couple of you guys. So they ran, Chet passed away about two years ago and the company's kind of slowed down and they don't really are running it anymore but for years they ran these radio ads. It was Chet and Tony talking about, here's the greatest way in the world to double your sales, call in for this free report. And they run these during drive time radio. Anyone know why they'd run it during drive time radio? Traffic butt. More so than just traffic. They were targeting a very specific person who they knew was going to be in the car during drive times listening to satellite radio. Executives. Executives. Boom. That's what they wanted. Their average client was making $1 in $3 million a year. That's who they were trying to target. So for them, same kind of thing. What was their bait? It was these reports. Where were they looking for these people? They were on radio driving their car listening to satellite radio. Same stuff we're talking about online and offline. They're the same thing. They didn't run these radio ads and you would hear the radio and you'd call in to get your free report and when the call in, a sales person pick up the phone and say, hey, what's your email address? Send you the report. And the sales person would say, hey, by the way, Chet uses these events in Vegas. They were $1,000 a day to come to these events. People loved them. But a lot of the executives like you are too busy to come to our events and so we started doing web-based events. Excuse me, there's one coming up this week. It's three hours long. We're going to learn everything that Chet knows about how to double your sales. We'd love to have you on this webinar. We'd like to come on it. The guy's like, sure, I'd love to. He's like, cool, we'd love to have you on it. This is the deal. The webinar is not free. It's a $300 webinar, $297 webinar. But we want to make sure this is worth it to you. So we're not going to charge you anything right now. You can come for free. And at the end of the webinar, if you like it, let us know and we'll bill you $300. If you think it sucks, just tell us we won't bill you anything. Does that sound fair? The guy's like, all right, sure. So the guy goes, he gets his credit card, the sales person gets his credit card, he doesn't bill him, he gets his credit card on file and the person comes, listens to this training webinar. So he sends the webinar, if he likes it, then he sells them the $297 for the webinar. Pretty cool. And then from there, after they went through the 3-hour webinar, basically at the end of that, the person who did the webinar was like, so did you guys like this webinar? Was it awesome? Everyone's like, yeah. And then it says it was worth at least $200 or $500 a thousand. Builds up the value and says, OK, if you loved it, don't do anything, we'll bill you. If you didn't love it, email me and we won't bill you, right? And then the after that, he says, OK, now we got that taken care of. This was not a webinar where we were going to sell you anything. But for some of you guys, obviously you're interested in what else we have to offer, right? What other value can we provide your business? So if you don't want to know, you can hang up right now. But if you're interested, I'm going to tell you really quick about our next program. And so the guy gets on and does a really quick 15-minute pitch for their $4,000 program, their 4K program. What's happening? We're going to bring him up the ladder, right? Now this $4,000 program is a big huge home study course with four coaching calls to help them to consume this course. So they get this big box of DVDs. Boom, first coaching call happens, second coaching call happens, third coaching call happens. Guess what happens on the fourth coaching call? Hey, was this valuable to you? Because guess what else we got? We got this thing for 9K that we billed out, what do they call it? Your stadium pitch. Boom. And from there, he's got one that's $30K a month and they keep sending people out through there. The first thing I learned from Chet and what they were doing is that every single thing they sell, it pre-sells the next thing, which pre-sells the next thing, which pre-sells the next thing. The only thing you think about in your business is whatever you're selling should be kind of pre-selling the next thing. It's really powerful, right? This thing that they just went through buys next thing and the next thing. But the piece that was really powerful about this whole invisible funnel was this right here. And so I saw this from these guys and I'm like, this is awesome. I want to do the same thing, but I couldn't figure out quite how to replicate it. And so one day I was doing a call with my buddy Dagen Smith and I was kind of explaining these guys' model to me. He's like, that's awesome. He's like, I want to do that. I'm like, I do too, but I didn't quite figure out how to replicate that perfectly online. And so we never really did anything. And then a couple years ago, Dagen and I were at an event and it's kind of a funny story. We're sitting out at lunch, eating lunch, and we're having a good time. And then we get done eating and we get up and we walk back in the event and both of us forgot to pay. And we sit down at the event and he leans over and says, hey, thanks for the food. Thanks for paying for lunch. I was like, I thought you paid for lunch. He's like, you didn't pay? I'm like, no. We're like, oh crap, we got to pay. So we jump back up, run out, we find our server, we're like, hey man, we forgot to pay. I'm so sorry. And it turns out that Brent actually, wherever he's at, Brent actually paid for us. We're like, oh, good. At least somebody paid. We didn't just stiff the guy. And so we go back in the room. We're sitting there and Dagen's got this look on his face. You can tell he's thinking and trying to figure some stuff out. And then he leans over to me and he's like, I think I got it. I'm like, got what? I think I figured out how to do Tony and Chet's thing online. I was like, what do you mean? I'll show you. Just look for my emails this week. You'll see what happens. I'm like, all right. So we go back and a couple days later, here I start seeing these emails from Dagen talking about this epiphany he had while sitting at a restaurant last weekend and all this stuff. And then I click on the link and I go to this page. And basically the page is like, hey, I'm doing this live training. It's going to be awesome. It's a $97 training, but I'm only guys coming for free because what happened this weekend was really cool. Actually, well, you can tell I'll probably ask. You come in for free, put your credit card in, and then I won't bill you until after the webinar is over. And if you love it, then we'll do it. So I saw it and I was like, oh, that's how you that's it made it. It was like so clear at that point. It's like perfect. He figured out how to do it. So he did it, ran this campaign, made a ton of money. And then we did it about two weeks later. I tested out the exact same thing. And I'll kind of show you our stats the very first one we did because it worked out awesome. But that's kind of the concept behind the invisible funnel. Dagen's big a ha, I think he told me is like, after we left dinner, after we had left lunch, we were sitting there, he said, look how guilty we both felt that we hadn't paid. We'd receive all this value. And the law reciprocity was so high that we felt so guilty we had to go back in and pay that person money because we felt that. He says, what if we did that in this event where we give him so much amazing stuff, it's like they kind of, they feel this guilt, I need to pay you. I feel like you gave me so much, I have to pay you back. That's kind of what re-sparked this idea in his head and where he kind of invented this whole concept. So we ran our very first invisible funnel. It was called affiliate boot camp. Anyone here was on that? A couple of you guys? It was awesome. So we did it, we promoted it. We had 550 people signed up for it and we did, it was a dollar for our first one and then afterwards we were going to bill $47. Okay? 550 people. So that's what happened. A webinar happened and typically when you do like a free webinar, excuse me, if you can get about 30% of the people who sign up to actually show up, that's really good thing. If I get 1,000 people to register, if I get 30 people to show up on the webinar, I'm fired up and excited, right? So I was expecting 550 people, maybe about 30% will actually show up. So I did this webinar. It was a Friday afternoon. It started at one in the afternoon and it went for four hours. I taught all of my best stuff for four hours long. What was crazy was of these 550 people, 85% showed up. 85% showed up and they were sitting there. So now just by the nature of me doing this direction, I had more than double as many people I could sell to, which was awesome. Then I got on this thing for four hours. I didn't try to sell anything. I just gave my as much as I could, my best content, as much as I could, just gave, gave, gave, gave and just didn't try to hold anything back and just share it and just try to provide as much value as humanly possible. And at the end of the webinar, I was like, hey guys, so you know how it worked when you signed up? Basically, if you think this is awesome, don't do anything and tomorrow I'm going to bill you $47. And if you didn't like it, just email me, let me know and we won't bill you. And I was like super scared, like I'm going to get 500 emails right now and I'm going to waste four hours of my life. Of these 550 people, we only had about 10% cancel. So about 50 people cancel or so. We spent 500 people-ish, got billed $47. Who knows the math on that? This is money math. This is an easy one. It's pretty like that's a five. That's how I do my math. 25 grand. So I made 25 grand just showing up and teaching my best stuff. But it gets even better than that. Because after I told them that, I was like, hey guys, if you're interested, we've got this really cool done for you program, top of the value ladder, where everything I just showed you for the last four hours, I can just do it all for you. And if you're interested, it's five grand, just go over here and pay right over here. And I don't remember the exact stats that happened there, but we had a whole slew of people come over here and pay us an extra 5k. I don't remember the number, we did make- we more than doubled how much money we made here from people buying this back end thing. And literally the pitch for that might have been maybe 60 seconds long. It was literally like, hey, all that stuff I showed you guys think that was pretty cool? Because if you want, we've got a team of people who are going to do this for you. It's $5,000. Go to this page just so we sign up to get that thing done. Looking back now, I could have pitched that a lot better, but that was all I did. And even with that, we made an extra 25. I think it was more than that. But whatever extra money we made right there. And so that is kind of what the invisible funnel is. That make sense, you guys? So we start right here where we drive traffic to a page. And typically we do invisible funnels live. We've also been now automating our invisible funnels just so that I can not have to be there all the time. They come in, they register for webinar, it's $0 up front and you're going to be able to build something afterwards. In the make money space, we saw no drop off bumping it from $47 to $97. Another market, obviously you can't always do that, but we didn't see any big decrease. So a couple people were trying to do two payments of $97 and it just didn't work because the story is confusing at that point. It's kind of weird. But going 0 to 97 works really good in our market. Afterwards they come on this webinar where for two or three or four hours, however long it takes to teach your best stuff, you just give like crazy on that. And at the end of it you offer to top your value ladder, whatever that thing might be. In a business perspective, one of our friends is in one of our mastermind groups, her name is Brittany. She's in the weight loss space. And typically in the weight loss space, people always say you can't sell more than $50 or $100 in weight loss space. People don't sell expensive stuff. And it's been always kind of like just the thought that you can't sell a $2 or $3,000 product for weight loss. And I always believe that. It's like people won't pay that. But she totally proved this wrong. She went back, we did an event, day and night, a three day long event, teaching this entire concept. And while we were at the event, after the second night she went to her hotel room and she launched one of these really quick. She didn't have a webinar so she just did an invisible final tele-seminar. She got a tele-seminar for four hours, gave her best stuff as much as she could. At the end of it she had everyone on their head, if you're interested in my high-end coaching program, email my assistant right here. She emailed her assistant. Her assistant next day took all these emails and started calling every single person back. And over the course of the weekend we were there, she made over $80,000 in back end sales selling her $2,500 how to lose weight digital coaching program. Which by the way was six tele-seminars, they got to listen into her talking about how to lose weight. So this concept works awesome, even in markets you don't think it would work. One of our businesses in the couponing market, which by the way is the worst market to get into because nobody wants to pay things. The nature of the customer, they don't want to spend money, right? And so we kept trying all these things, nobody would buy anything. We were like, there's got to be a way to sell these guys something. And the invisible funnel worked. Made an invisible funnel where our couponing girl named Jessica Hacker, she comes in and basically says, I'm going to show you guys how to coupon. And it's free, you're going to come in, you're going to go through this program. And at the end of it I'm going to show you how to save like $100 on your grocery bill. And then basically you're going to learn how to save $100 on your grocery bill. And then she comes back and says, if you don't save $100 on your grocery bill, then let me know and I won't bill you anything. But if you do, then I'm just going to bill you $20. So I'm basically going to give you $100 in exchange for $20. And it's worked great. We also did this in the speed reading market. One of my friends is Howard Berg, he's the world's fastest reader. Anyone here have ever seen Howard read before? This is like the greatest thing you will ever watch. If you go to YouTube and type in Howard Berg, world's fastest reader, there's a video of him, he was on Fox News and he read the entire health care bill live in 50 minutes in front of all of America. He read the whole thing and then Cavuto started quizzing him on everything and he was able to repeat back everything that he had said. He came to our office a little while ago and Mike's partner Mandy brought her book and he, we sat down and he sat there and he read her entire book in what, five, six minutes, read her whole book and the man he started quizzing him and he knew the answer to every single thing she said. It's the coolest thing you will ever see. And so when he hadn't come to our office, I was like, I want you to teach me and everybody else how to increase the reading. So we took our entire company through the process and I think the worst person in the room doubled their reading in four hours. Most people were five, six, seven times as fast. It was the coolest thing in the world. So we created Invisible Funnel and this thing got the Invisible Funnel. You get the right hook, these things just blow up like crazy. So our hook for him was like, hey, it's called doubleyeareding.com. And it was like, Howard Berg will teach you how to double your reading in three hours or you pay nothing. So come on the scene, put your credit card and we'll charge you a penny. You show up, you go through this process, you take a test at the beginning, at the end, if you haven't doubled your reading, then we'll give your money back. If you have doubled it, don't do anything, we'll bill you 97 bucks. Okay, we did that and it worked awesome. Okay, did you see how you get the right hook? Another one, we used to be in the, I had this guy that we were working with in the dating market and he is, how do I say this nicely? You could probably someday watch this video, I feel bad. He is not a good looking guy by any stretch of the imagination, right? So the big hook is like, look at me, I'm overweight, not very good looking, I get really good looking girls and I'm going to take you guys through a process I go through and literally you sign up on Friday, you can go through my webinars three hours long, you learn my process to go pick up chicks and then when the webinar is over, Friday night you go out to the bar, you practice my stuff, you try it and I guarantee you you will get a hot chick. If you don't get a hot chick, email me before Monday and I'll cancel your bill. If you do get a good looking girl, don't do anything and I'll charge you the next day. That one worked really good as well. And so for you guys in your business, the Visible Funnel works really good. If you've got a process you can take somebody through, especially like a Howard Berkley, I'm going to take you through this process and you can get this result, right? Go through this three hours and you're going to double your reading. Go through this process and you'll get a girlfriend this weekend. Go through this process and you're going to lose five pounds, you're going to detox. Go through it, you know, whatever that thing is, if you have something fast like that that you can tie this to, Visible Funnel works like crazy. Like I said, it's a great pre-sell to get people now into your high ticket back in stuff. Any questions about the Visible Funnel at all? Exactly, so the guarantee is, I guarantee you this result or else you don't pay anything. And the reality, if they don't get the result, most of them don't cancel anyway because usually they blame themselves. But if you have a product, you can give them a result like that really, really quick. Man, it's like the most powerful thing ever.
